sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: KRC Genk have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches with 2 wins and 2 losses, scoring an average of 1.8 goals per game. SV Zulte Waregem have struggled, with only 1 win in their last 5 matches, conceding an average of 1.8 goals per game.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last 2 head-to-head matches, KRC Genk have dominated SV Zulte Waregem, winning both encounters with a combined score of 5-1. Genk also have a strong historical record, winning all 5 of the last 5 matchups.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: KRC Genk rank higher in key attacking metrics like ball possession (63.5%) and penalty area entries (34.75 per game), while SV Zulte Waregem struggle defensively, conceding more goals (9) in the season so far.

sections.subtitle: Team Streaks and Trends
sections.text: Genk have scored first in 5 of their last 7 home games and have a strong record of over 2.5 goals in head-to-head matches. Zulte Waregem have failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 7 away games.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between KRC Genk and SV Zulte Waregem ended in an exciting 2-2 draw at the Cegeka Arena. Both teams showed attacking intent early on, with goals coming in quick succession. Genk struck first through Robin Mirisola and Mujaid Sadick, while Zulte Waregem responded with strikes from Jeppe Erenbjerg and a penalty by Anton Tanghe. The scoreline reflects the competitive nature of the game, with both sides having moments of dominance. However, Genk’s red card to Sadick could prove to be a turning point as they head into the second half with a numerical disadvantage.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: KRC Genk dominated possession and created more opportunities in the first half, frequently entering the final third and penalty area with purpose. Their ability to win duels and aerial battles gave them an edge in controlling the game’s tempo. Despite this, their defensive lapse and the red card have left them vulnerable. On the other hand, SV Zulte Waregem made the most of their limited possession, capitalizing on key moments to level the score. Their efficiency in front of goal and ability to exploit set pieces, like the penalty, kept them in the contest despite being outplayed in midfield.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: The game started with Genk showing early attacking intent, quickly gaining the upper hand with a series of forward surges. Zulte Waregem responded with a spell of pressure, pushing back to create their own chances. However, Genk soon regained control, enjoying a prolonged period of dominance in the attacking third with consistent waves of pressure. Towards the middle of the half, momentum swung briefly to Zulte Waregem as they mounted a comeback. The latter stages saw Genk reassert themselves with renewed vigor, while Zulte Waregem had fleeting moments of threat before the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Genk, Robin Mirisola has been a standout performer, finding the net and posing a constant threat upfront. His ability to position himself for chances could be crucial in the second half. Jarne Steuckers and Zakaria El Ouahdi have also contributed creatively, and their influence will be vital with a man down. For Zulte Waregem, Jeppe Erenbjerg’s goal and overall impact make him a player to watch, while Anton Tanghe’s composure from the spot highlights his importance in critical moments. Their attacking duo will likely look to exploit Genk’s reduced numbers.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical outlook for second half
halftimeSections.text: With Genk down to ten men, they are likely to adopt a more cautious approach, focusing on compact defending while seeking counter-attacking opportunities. Their superior possession stats suggest they can still dictate play if they manage spaces well. Zulte Waregem, sensing an opportunity, may push forward more aggressively to capitalize on the numerical advantage. Expect them to target the flanks and test Genk’s makeshift defense. Set pieces could also play a significant role for both sides, given the first-half trends.
